title: SECTION 1
content: Company Vision & Growth

title: Our Story
content:
FROM SIDE HUSTLE TO INDUSTRY LEADER

Contractors Ignite began when Mani and Oscar, then just 15 years old, set out to help their families by using their skills to support local contractors.

What started as a small side project quickly grew into a mission: empower deserving contractors to scale beyond what they thought possible.

Today, that same drive fuels our entire team. Every campaign, every lead, and every appointment we generate is part of a bigger goal: to help contracting companies break growth ceilings and achieve lasting success.

title: From RoofIgnite to Contractors Ignite
content:
Our Evolution
  • Founded as RoofIgnite (roofing-focused)
  • Expanded to multi-niche contractor marketing
  • Current: Roofing, HVAC, Gutters
  • Future: Additional contractor trades
Why the Expansion?
  • Proven systems work across niches
  • Diversification strengthens business
  • Larger addressable market
  • Same performance-based model, different industries

title: Where We Are & Where We're Going
content:
CURRENT STATE
$200K/mo
  • Multiple contractor verticals
  • Operations infrastructure built
  • Proven acquisition systems
→
TARGET STATE
$1M/mo
  • Aggressive scaling phase
  • Focus on retention & performance
  • World-class operations team
Your Role is Critical to This Mission

title: Retention > Acquisition
content:
Every additional cycle = compounding revenue

×

title: SECTION 2
content: The Contractor Industry

title: Understanding the Contractor Industry
content:
We serve three primary verticals: Roofing, HVAC, Gutters

YOU MUST UNDERSTAND:
Industry terminologySales languageMarketing languageSeasonality patternsCustomer behaviorContractor pain points

title: Roofing Industry 101
content:
What is Roofing?

Residential and commercial roof replacements/repairs — our primary focus is residential roof replacements (highest ticket).

Key Stats
  • Average job: $8,000–$30,000
  • Sweet spot: $15,000–$25,000
  • Sales cycle: 7–21 days
  • Measured in squares (100 sq ft)
Sales Types
RetailInsuranceFinanced
Materials
ShinglesMetalClayTile

title: Roofing Sales Language
content:
RETAIL

Customer pays out of pocket (full amount)

INSURANCE

Insurance covers roof, customer pays deductible ($20K roof → $1–3K out of pocket)

FINANCED

Customer needs a payment plan (applies to both retail and insurance)

Sales Cycle: Typically 7–21 days from lead to close

title: Roofing Marketing Language
content:
Offline Marketing (competitors use)
  • Referrals (word of mouth)
  • Door knocking / Canvassing
  • Storm chasing
Online Marketing
  • Facebook/Instagram ads — our focus
  • Recycled lead providers (Angi, HomeAdvisor)
Our Advantage: We generate exclusive leads — never recycled, never shared

title: Roofing Industry Slang
content:
Chuck-in-a-Truck

Low-level contractor who underbids everyone. Competes solely on price. Not our ICP.

Tire-Kicker

A lead with no urgency, no buying intent, or no money. What we filter out through our qualification survey.

Storm Chaser

Roofer who follows damaged areas. Reactive, not strategic. We typically avoid — they rely on insurance only.

title: HVAC Industry 101
content:
What is HVAC?

Heating, Ventilation, Air Conditioning for residential & commercial properties.

Key Stats
  • Average replacement: $3,000–$15,000
  • Faster sales cycles (urgency-driven)
  • Highly seasonal (summer/winter peaks)
Critical Success Factor
Speed to Lead
5-minute VA response for emergencies

title: HVAC Sales & Marketing
content:
Sales Dynamics
  • High urgency (especially emergencies)
  • Faster decision-making than roofing
  • Price sensitivity varies by emergency level
Marketing Focus
  • Emergency positioning in ads
  • Trust and speed emphasis
  • Reviews critical for conversion
Our Role: Generate qualified leads fast + ensure 5-min VA response rate

title: Gutter Industry 101
content:
What are Gutters?

Rain drainage systems. Often bundled with roofing — natural upsell opportunity.

Key Stats
  • Average job: $1,000–$5,000 (lowest ticket)
  • Quick close cycles (on-the-spot decisions)
  • Spring/Fall seasonality
Success Factor

Volume business — efficiency and lead quantity over quality. Need 2–3× the leads vs. roofing to hit same revenue.

title: Niche-Specific Success Factors
content:
🏠 ROOFING
  • Creative quality (trust-building)
  • Longer nurture sequences
  • Higher price justification
❄️ HVAC
  • Speed to lead critical
  • Emergency positioning in ads
  • Seasonal campaign adjustments
🌧 GUTTERS
  • High volume lead generation
  • Fast qualification process
  • Efficient booking systems

title: Homeowner Psychology (B2C)
content:
UPPER CLASS
  • Get 2–3 quotes
  • Quality > Price
  • Choose based on trust & reputation
MIDDLE CLASS ← OUR TARGET
  • Get 3 quotes
  • Best overall value wins
  • Mix of quality and price
LOWER CLASS (not our ICP)
  • Get 5+ quotes
  • Choose cheapest

title: SECTION 3
content: Our Business Model & Offer

title: How We Structure Our Offer
content:
SETUP FEE (One-time)
  • Client pays upfront: $1,500–$5,000
  • We build: landing pages, ads, CRM, automations
  • Timeline: 5–10 business days
28-DAY PERFORMANCE CYCLES
  • Client pays ONLY if targets are hit
  • Targets based on daily ad spend
  • We generate qualified booked appointments
  • Performance-based = skin in the game

title: The 28-Day Cycle Model
content:
Ad Spend$100/day = $2,800/cycle
Target15 qualified booked appointments
80% RuleMinimum 12 appointments to bill
Miss Target?Extend cycle at no charge (max ~42 days)
Retainer~$4,000/cycle + 10% of ad spend
Billing TimingClient pays for the previous cycle, not current

title: Why This Model Works
content:
For Clients
  • No risk (pay for results only)
  • Predictable appointment flow
  • Continuous optimization
For Us
  • Forces accountability
  • Creates recurring revenue
  • Incentivizes retention
  • Compounds over time
For You
  • Your job = ensure clients hit targets
  • Keep them on multiple cycles
  • Commission compounds with retention

title: Winter Program & Peak Seasons
content:
🌞 PEAK SEASON (Spring–Fall)

Full service, aggressive scaling, maximum volume. This is when clients make their year's revenue.

❄️ WINTER PROGRAM (Nov–Feb)

Reduced retainer (~$1,500/cycle). Brand awareness ads only ($10–50/day). Keep pixel warm. Prevent starting from scratch in spring.

See: Winter Retainer Plan SOP

title: Key Franchises We Work With
content:
Klaus Roofing Systems (KRS)

Nationwide roofing franchise. We have 10 direct KRS partners. Special retainer: $3,300/cycle (vs $4,000 standard).

Gutter Shutter

Gutter protection franchise system. Same discounted $3,300 retainer as KRS.

Material Providers

GAF & Owens Corning — you'll see their branding on client landing pages.

content:
20% of inputs drive 80% of results
1
Ad Creative Quality → Drives cost per lead
2

4

5

title: The A, B, C, D Contractor Tiers
content:
A-TIER ($2.5M+/yr) ← PRIMARY TARGET

Business owners who rely on their company. Value time, transparency, systems. Value-based thinkers.

B-TIER ($1M–$2.5M/yr) ← ACCEPTABLE

Managers of other people. Company relies on them. Growth-focused and coachable.

C-TIER ($500K–$1M/yr) — AVOID

Contractors who grew but are stuck. Need systems to scale. Often emotionally reactive.

D-TIER ($0–$500K/yr) — AVOID

One-man-show operators. Emotional decision-makers. Not ready for our model.

title: Ideal Client Profile (ICP)
content:
✅ $1M+ annual revenue
✅ Established (3+ years in business)
✅ Growth-minded owner
✅ Willing to invest $3K+/month in ad spend
✅ Responsive and communicative
✅ Trusts experts (not a micromanager)
A-players want to work with A-players. Our positioning reflects this.

title: Contractor Psychology (B2B)
content:
What Contractors Think
  • "Facebook leads suck"
  • "I've tried marketing before — it didn't work"
  • "Angi's List leads are garbage"
Our Positioning
  • Extensive social proof (success stories)
  • Performance-based model (low risk)
  • Only work with established businesses
  • High authority positioning — it's an honor to work with us

title: SECTION 6
content: Service Delivery Flow

title: The Client Journey — Overview
content:
PHASE 1 Lead comes in (B2B ads)
PHASE 2 Sales call (Mani's team)
PHASE 3 Client closed, assigned to pod
PHASE 4 Onboarding call — YOU
PHASE 5 Service delivery setup (Emmanuel, 5–10 days)
PHASE 6 Campaign launch
PHASE 7 Ongoing optimization — YOU coordinate
PHASE 8 Cycle completion & billing
PHASE 9+ Renewal and next cycle
You enter at Phase 4 and own through Phase 9+

title: Phase 4: Onboarding Call
content:
Your Goals
  • Understand their business deeply
  • Gather all assets (logos, photos, brand)
  • Set clear timeline expectations
  • Explain the 28-day cycle model
  • Establish communication cadence
  • Create excitement and confidence
Post-Call Actions (within 1 hour)
  • Paste Fathom transcript into Custom GPT
  • Post to #post-onboarding-discussion Slack
  • Create ClickUp task for Emmanuel with deadline
  • Text client confirming next steps
  • Add to Client Check-In Sheet
First impression = everything. You are the authority.

title: Phase 5: Service Delivery Setup
content:
WHOEmmanuel (+ Mervin for additional setups)
TIMELINE5–10 business days
WHAT GETS BUILTLanding pages, surveys, GHL account, CRM automations, Meta campaigns, VA assignment, A2P registration
YOUR ROLECoordinate, chase blockers, ensure deadlines are met — every day of delay = delayed cashflow
A2PALWAYS Emmanuel via A2P Wizard. NEVER submit yourself. Rejection = 2–3 week delay.
Reference: R.LTD Service Delivery SOP + GHL Build recording for full process

title: APIs & Zapier Integrations
content:
When clients have their own CRMs:

1Identify client's existing CRM system
2Set up Google Sheet lead master (always done)
3Set up Zapier: new row in Google Sheet → create contact in client's CRM
4Test data flow in both directions
5Monitor for errors and sync issues
Why Google Sheet as intermediary: GHL → any CRM via Zapier is cleaner than direct integration

title: Phase 6-7: Launch & Optimization
content:
Launch
  • Campaigns go live
  • VAs begin responding to leads
  • Client starts receiving appointments
  • Send approval Loom to client first
Ongoing Optimization
  • Creative refresh (Ken — AI Creative)
  • Campaign adjustments (Emmanuel/Mervin)
  • VA training (Leila/Aica — VA Managers)
  • Landing page tweaks (Emmanuel)
Your role: Diagnose problems and coordinate solutions — you do not execute the tactics

title: Phase 8-9: Cycle Completion
content:
Cycle Completion
  • Verify target was hit (or 80% rule met)
  • Prepare billing — client pays for completed cycle
  • Log in Account Master Dashboard
Renewal
  • Celebrate wins proactively
  • Address any concerns head-on
  • Secure commitment for next cycle (5–7 days before end)
  • Set new targets if ad spend changes
Your Goal: Keep clients on as many cycles as possible — every renewal = compounding commission

title: SECTION 9
content: KPI Diagnosis Playbook

title: You Are a Performance Doctor
content:
1Diagnose the root cause
↓
2Prescribe the solution
↓
3Coordinate specialists to execute
↓
4Follow up to verify improvement
Think in systems, not emotions. Everything is a numbers game.

title: Understanding Metrics Hierarchy
content:
LAYER 1 — CHECK FIRST
MOST IMPORTANT
  • Overall Bookings
  • Target Achievement %
  • Cost Per Booked Appointment
  • Quantity of Booked Appointments
✓ If good → Don't worry about Layer 2
LAYER 2 — ONLY IF LAYER 1 FAILS
DIAGNOSTIC TOOLS
  • Click-Through Rate (CTR)
  • Cost Per Link Click (CPLC)
  • Lead Conversion Rate
  • Survey conversion rate
  • VA pickup rate & speed
⚠ Use to improve Layer 1

title: The Diagnostic Approach
content:
Layer 1 Metrics Looking Good?
YES ✓

Keep Going! No need to worry about Layer 2. Monthly check-in only.

NO ✗

Diagnose Layer 2. Look at CTR, CPLC, conversion rate. Improve Layer 2 to fix Layer 1.

title: Problem #1: Low Survey Conversion
content:
SYMPTOMSHigh landing page traffic, low survey completions (below 2–3%)
DIAGNOSISSurvey too long, confusing language, or friction in questions
PRESCRIPTIONSwitch 7-question → 4-question survey. Simplify language. A/B test variations.
WHO EXECUTESEmmanuel (setup change) — you task via ClickUp

title: Problem #2: High Cost Per Lead
content:
SYMPTOMSSpending too much per lead generated. CPL rising week over week.
DIAGNOSISAd creative fatigue (frequency rising, CTR dropping) or targeting mismatch
PRESCRIPTIONCreative refresh (new visuals + copy). Task Ken via ClickUp with brand info + market. Adjust targeting.
WHO EXECUTESKen (creatives) + Emmanuel/Mervin (campaign) — you task via ClickUp

title: Problem #3: Low Quality Leads
content:
SYMPTOMSLots of leads, but VAs report unqualified prospects. High out-of-service-area rate.
DIAGNOSISPixel not seasoned, targeting too broad, or radius mismatch (Account Specific Doc vs. Meta vs. actual)
PRESCRIPTIONAudit all 3 sources for service area discrepancies. Use Claude/ChatGPT to compare. Add exclusion zip codes. Advanced pixel conditioning.
WHO EXECUTESEmmanuel/Mervin (targeting) — you audit and task via ClickUp

title: Problem #4: Low Booking Rate
content:
SYMPTOMSLeads come in but don't convert to booked appointments
DIAGNOSISVA performance issues, script problems, or slow response time (should be <5 min)
PRESCRIPTIONFlag to Leila/Aica with specific lead examples. Request call recording review. Script retraining.
WHO EXECUTESLeila / Aica (VA managers)

title: Problem #5: Low Show Rate
content:
SYMPTOMSAppointments booked but clients don't show up for inspections
DIAGNOSISReminder system failure, poor qualification, or long gap between booking and appointment
PRESCRIPTIONReminder automation adjustment (SMS/email). Confirmation process improvement. Requalification of leads.
WHO EXECUTESEmmanuel (CRM automation) + Leila/Aica (VA follow-up)

title: SECTION 10
content: Culture & Performance

title: Our Operating Rhythm
content:
MON + THU
Post KPI updates for your pod in #ops-manager-discussion

Clear, concise, data-driven. Celebrate wins, flag problems. Include specific action steps with dates.

TUE + FRI
Review calls with Jonathan

Discuss pod performance. Surface blockers. Coordinate solutions. Come prepared — action steps ready before the call.

This rhythm is non-negotiable.

title: Daily Expectations
content:
Morning
  • "Good morning" in #internal-team Slack
  • Review overnight client messages
  • Triage and prioritize
Throughout Day
  • Active responsiveness (5–30 min response)
  • Proactive client communication
  • Coordinate with specialists as needed
  • Chase all open blockers
Evening
  • "Good night" in #internal-team Slack
  • Ensure nothing critical is unresolved
  • Set tomorrow's priorities
No disappearing acts. Radical ownership.

title: The A-Player Standard
content:
Performance Culture
  • We hire slow, fire fast
  • Hit your numbers → incentives, bonuses, growth
  • Miss your numbers → you're not meeting our standard
Feedback Philosophy
  • No ego, only results
  • Feedback is a gift, not an attack
  • Coachability is required — always
Core Value

Radical ownership of your pod and your outcomes. No excuses. No waiting to be told.

title: SECTION 11
content: Tools & Systems

title: Setup Tools (You Coordinate)
content:
GoHighLevel (GHL)

Central hub for all client funnels. Automations, workflows, pipelines, phone system, logbook integration.

Meta Ads Manager

Where campaigns live and run. Your performance data source for Layer 2 diagnosis.

Cloudflare (DNS)

Domain management and security. Landing page hosting.

Porkbun (Domains)

Domain purchasing and registration. Create your own account — Oscar invites you to team.

title: Daily Operations Tools
content:
Slack

All internal communication. Client update channels. Team coordination. Your primary hub.

ClickUp

Project management and task tracking. Assign setup tasks to Emmanuel, creative tasks to Ken. Track onboarding stages.

Google Workspace

Email, docs, sheets, drive. Shared folder structures. Documentation and SOPs.

Fathom

AI meeting recorder. Must be ON for all onboarding calls. Transcript feeds into Custom GPT for post-call summaries.

title: Additional Tools
content:
BambooHR

HR management. Primarily for VAs — minimal use for pod managers.

Intuit / QuickBooks

Financial systems. Oscar manages. Coordinate with Oscar for direct deposit setup.

Canva

Creative asset edits. Ken uses primarily — awareness only for you.

Loom

Async walkthroughs. Create your own free account. Use for internal setup review videos and client approval Looms.

title: Our Internal Software Stack
content:
Logbook

Client interaction tracking & history. Central truth for all lead data. Access from Leila.

Account Master Dashboard

Real-time account performance & cycle metrics. Cole's system — updated regularly.

Command Center

Health score dashboard. Color-coded: 🟢 Healthy / 🟡 Watch / 🟠 Act Now / 🔴 Escalate.

Custom GPT

Paste Fathom transcript → structured onboarding notes for ClickUp & #post-onboarding-discussion.

title: Your Tech Proficiency Timeline
content:
Day 1
Must Be Fluent: Slack, ClickUp, Google Workspace
Week 1
Must Understand: GoHighLevel navigation and structure
Week 2
Must Read: Meta Ads Manager data and KPIs
Week 3+
Must Understand: How all systems work together — Logbook → Dashboard → Command Center
Training is provided. Learning speed is expected. Ask questions proactively.
